description:
Today TaskType is a fixed enum: Epic, Task, Bug (plus Unknown for forward-compat). Beads ships feature/chore/spike/question/discussion/retro etc. In balls, type is purely a label ('not a behavior switch' per SKILL.md) so widening the vocabulary is design, not code.

Direction: make type an open-ended string. Optionally register allowed types in .balls/config.json; anything outside the declared set emits a warning but is still accepted (forward-compat). No declared set means all strings go.

Implementation sketch:
- Replace TaskType enum with a newtype around String, with a parse() that enforces [a-z][a-z0-9_-]* or similar filesystem/git-safe rules.
- Keep TaskType::Unknown semantics via the wrapper: an unknown type is just a string that isn't in the declared set; still round-trips.
- Config.toml-or-json gains an optional task_types array. If present, bl create validates against it and warns on mismatch.
- bl create -t accepts any string that passes the regex. Help text lists the common set (task, bug, epic, feature, chore, spike, question, discussion, retro) as suggestions, not constraints.
- Existing TaskType::Epic etc. map to the string 'epic', 'task', 'bug'. On-disk format unchanged — it already serializes as a string.

Forward-compat:
- Older bl reading a newer file with a novel type string lands it in TaskType::Unknown(s) via the existing passthrough and round-trips it unchanged. Already works today if we replace the enum carefully.
- Newer bl reading older files: identical, types are strings.

Open questions:
- Do we want a 'strict' flag in config that rejects unknown types instead of warning?
- Where does the suggested-vocabulary list live? Hardcoded in help, or loaded from config for discoverability?

Not in scope:
- Type-specific behavior (e.g. 'epic auto-gates children'). Types remain pure labels.
- Migration of existing repos — types are already strings on disk.
title: Open-ended task types with optional per-repo declared vocabulary
